body:
| **[Take our BOLD Election Survey to let us know what you think about the GOP Presidential Primary so far>>](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsH/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3")**

[Tomorrow](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsE/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3") night, the leading Republican Presidential candidates will gather for their last debate of 2015.

So far, these candidates have been entirely focused on firing up their base using inflammatory and downright hateful rhetoric.

**[But whoever wins the Republican primary will go head-to-head with our Democratic nominee in the General Election.**So we need your feedback**](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsF/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3")**[ _today_](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsC/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3") on these GOP candidates:**** | ******[Take our short survey>>](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsD/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3")******
---
Friends --

The GOP primary so far has been a shameful competition over inflaming the Republican base with racist, divisive rhetoric -- while ignoring the crucial issues facing our country today.

So we aren't expecting too much from [tomorrow](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsA/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3") night's debate, but here's what we know:

**Will they discuss real ways we can prevent gun violence?_Doubtful._
Can we expect a conversation about solutions to climate change? _No._
Will they talk about fixing our broken immigration system? _NOPE._
How about protecting women's access to health care? _NOT A CHANCE!_**

Instead, we should probably prepare for more of this:
[](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsB/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3")
---
Look, this is a crucial moment in the 2016 race. As we head into the Election Year, we need to have a clear picture of what we're up against.

That's why it's imperative we hear from our most active supporters like you to help guide our strategy in 2016.

[Please, will you take a minute to fill out our BOLD PAC Election Survey before tomorrow's Republican debate?](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsO/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3")

[**http://go.boldpac.com/Election-Survey**](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea7b9/e60883a/700454a0/14422acb/1019986440/VEsP/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/election-survey?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.14_B2_election-survey_X__F1_S3_C1__ns_T3")

Thanks for all your help,

_BOLD PAC_

\- December 14 2015 -
Donald J. Trump - Week In Review

| Dear Peter Willis, I wanted to be the first to share the latest national poll results from the highly respected [Monmouth University](http://email.donaldjtrump.com/t/r-l-vbuhdy-drdiuiutjh-j/). The results show me with 41% support and my next closest rival with 14%. Additionally, the just released [Quinnipiac poll](http://email.donaldjtrump.com/t/r-l-vbuhdy-drdiuiutjh-t/) shows me leading in Iowa with 28%. This is really exciting and a testament to your passion for my message and vision! It was another excellent week spent campaigning in the early primary states of Iowa, New Hampshire and South Carolina. On the anniversary of Pearl Harbor, I visited Charleston, South Carolina where we had an amazing rally aboard the USS Yorktown. Thousands came to hear me speak about the importance of our military and taking care of our great vets, homeland security, illegal immigration and much more. It was the largest crowd ever in the history of the ship. On Thursday, I visited New Hampshire for a meeting with the New England Police Benevolent Association. After the meeting, I received their coveted endorsement. I was joined by hundreds of officers, who are such amazing people. I am incredibly honored to receive this endorsement. My entire life has been spent defending the police and the incredible job they do. Especially today, they will play an increasingly vital part in making our nation safe. With their support and hard work together we will Make America Great Again. On Friday I was in Des Moines Iowa for a traditional town hall event at the Iowa State Fairgrounds. It was a wonderful crowd of people and I answered their important questions about local and national issues. I spoke about my faith, the need for strong borders and how I will create jobs--- something I have done all my life in the private sector.
_Speaking about the important issues with the people of Des Moines, Iowa_ On Saturday, we returned to South Carolina. Aiken County is beautiful and we had an incredible crowd of over 5,000 for a town hall discussion moderated by South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson. It was a great discussion and I was able to articulate my common sense solutions to the very serious problems our country is facing.
_A huge crowd in South Carolina!_ This will be another productive week! We are expecting thousands in Las Vegas tonight and tomorrow, be sure to tune in to CNN for the Republican Presidential debate at 8:50pm EST. On Wednesday, we will travel to Mesa, Arizona for a rally and Wednesday night I will have some fun with Jimmy Kimmel on ABC. I hope you enjoy it! We also had some amazing poll results last week showing that I continue to lead in almost all state and national surveys by wide margins! I appreciate your continued support and enthusiasm for my message, especially at this time of year. Wishing you all holiday blessings and look forward to seeing you on the campaign trail. With your support, we will Make America Great Again!
---

Donald J. Trump

[Forward to a friend](http://donaldtrumpcampaign.forwardtomyfriend.com/r-drdiuiutjh-E524705E-vbuhdy-l-o)

P.S. Visit [DonaldJTrump.com](http://email.donaldjtrump.com/t/r-l-vbuhdy-drdiuiutjh-i/) for gifts that will make your holiday great!

body:
Friend,

Tonight's the last debate in 2015 and I need your help.

[**I 've asked my team to pull the names of everyone who steps up before I take the stage and I want to see your name on the list. Will you chip in just $1 to let me know you have my back?**](http://click1.email.jeb2016.com/kpmkwrpqfsvjtztljrgkfjbzppjbqzwqkrdrvqzmmlrd_gtlrrnmw.html?a=62e094b3-a1ea-49d7-b595-267b8a1be5f1)

Since the last debate, the world's attention has once again shifted to our generational struggle against radical Islamic terrorism.

body:
Dear Patriot, By the time you read this, I'll be walking out on stage here in Las Vegas for the 5th and final Republican Debate of 2015. But I wanted to touch base with you one more time before I head out to ask for your most generous support. My team just put together a debate money bomb to rally support for tonight and it couldn't come at a more important time. With less than seven weeks until the Iowa Caucuses, the American people "zeroing in" on this Republican Primary Race for the Presidency like never before. [**I really need your support now. **](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32288355:W9C3cHyNJ:m:1:879035888:870110DE5A761FDEEDC0A305B3A724CE:r) | | [$10 MONEYBOMB >>](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32288355:W9C3cHyNJ:m:1:879035888:870110DE5A761FDEEDC0A305B3A724CE:r)

Most experts are predicting that terrorism will be the central focus of tonight's debate and I really can't think of a better topic.
After all, today is also Bill of Rights day.
**And I'm the ONLY candidate on stage tonight who will tell the American people that they can keep their liberty AND security.**
Sadly, many of my opponents don't agree. They sound like President Obama and Hillary Clinton telling us we need ever-growing government.
They tell us we need more boots on the ground in the Middle East -- even after we know from recent experience that hasn't worked.
They tell us they need the spy on each and every American citizen -- even when those programs haven't stopped one terrorist attack like the Boston bombing or San Bernardino.
Then, they tell us we need to turn a blind eye to our porous borders, ignore our broken visa system and spend our tax dollars for dangerous refugees from Syria and elsewhere [like I pointed out in my recent ad.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32288360:W9C3cHyNJ:m:1:879035888:870110DE5A761FDEEDC0A305B3A724CE:r)
Is all of this working?
Or should we get serious about securing our border, vetting people we allow into the country and surveilling the real terrorists -- instead of law-abiding American citizens?
**You know where I stand.**
Tonight, the American people will hear a new alternative to the status quo they're being fed by Big Government members of BOTH parties.
[I hope you can chip in and join me in showing the naysayers, we're here to stay.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32288355:W9C3cHyNJ:m:1:879035888:870110DE5A761FDEEDC0A305B3A724CE:r)
Your donation of any size would make a big difference.
[I'm counting on you to stand with me.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32288355:W9C3cHyNJ:m:1:879035888:870110DE5A761FDEEDC0A305B3A724CE:r)

Rand Paul

name: Jeff Weaver, Bernie 2016
subject: "I'm tired of being told by fellow Democrats that Bernie can't win."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
#
Friend -
I wanted to share a few of the stories from people who contributed yesterday in the hopes it would inspire you to do the same today.
> _" I donate a small amount every month to Bernie, but knowing that he's set this goal I wanted to donate a little more. He's the only candidate that I feel really represents the people. He's out here earning his support from the regular people of the United States. He's the only one who isn't being bought and sold by big corporations. And his political views and ways of running his campaigns aren't something new that he's doing just to woo voters, he has a long history of standing up for what is right (even if it was unpopular at the time)."_ \- Bailey from Oregon
Yesterday alone, over 25,000 people contributed to say they've had ENOUGH of the billionaire class buying our elections and our democracy. With just two weeks to go before our final FEC deadline of the year, Bernie really needs you to add yours today:
[**Add your $3 contribution today and we 'll reach our goal of 2 million individual contributions by midnight tomorrow.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb29b6/43f17890/4cc265cb/62161930/3944986708/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-get-2-million?refcode=em151215full")
Here are some more notes -- which one will inspire you to show your love for Bernie today?
Mick from West Virginia:
> _"I am a middle class union electrician, temporarily disabled from a workplace accident. I am so sick of watching every dime I make go to survival, unable to save for the future while the rich and CEOs are making record profits on my back. Bernie Sanders is the only politician I've been excited about and trust for years. If I had more to help this movement, I'd give more! "_
Katie from Washington, D.C.:
> _" I am a mother, wife and own my own business. I've enjoyed the freedom to pursue my dreams and I want to live in an America where others who don't have the advantages I did have an equal chance to succeed and pursue their dreams as well. I want to see more early childhood education, ample paid family leave, good schools and affordable or free college education. I want corporate interests out of politics. I want to work toward a healthier planet. I want sanity in our foreign policy. I see my values reflected in Bernie Sanders and his vision for America. I'm proud to support him for president."_
Ana from California:
> _" I came to this country as an international student from Brazil. I was 25 and my son was 8. Today my son and I are American citizens. I have a graduate degree and my son is applying for his graduate degree. We earned it with a lot of courage, hard work and integrity. We support Bernie for his same values, courage, hard work and integrity. We believe that he is the only candidate that can put us back on track."_
[**Join Katie, Bailey and Ana and make your $3 contribution to help our campaign reach its goal of 2 million individual contributions by midnight tomorrow.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb29b6/43f17890/4cc265cb/62161930/3944986708/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-get-2-million?refcode=em151215full")
Ellen from New York summed it up nicely with her contribution: "I'm tired of being told by fellow Democrats that Bernie can't win."
Like Bernie says, when people stand together, there is no limit to what we can together. And that includes winning the Democratic primary, the general election, and ushering in a political revolution that will take our country back from the billionaire class.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016

[](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb29b6/43f17890/4cc265cb/62161930/3944986708/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-get-2-million?refcode=em151215full")

name: Rand Paul
subject: They've learned nothing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Patriot,
There is no bigger question facing American
voters in 2016 than who will best protect America.
How we do it is the fundamental question of
this election.

Tonight's debate gave the American people a
crystal clear picture of the differences between my views and
those of my opponents.

I'm already hearing "you won the debate" from
every corner.

But the support I'm most interested in right
now is yours.

Will you please contribute to my campaign's
money bomb right away?

As you saw from tonight's debate, Marco Rubio
supports open borders and never-ending wars and government
spying. He has more allegiance to Chuck Schumer and the Gang of
Eight than conservative principles.

Chris Christie is clamoring for World War III
with reckless threats he has no intention of backing up. Donald
Trump wants to shut down the Internet without understanding what
that entails or the full frontal attack on the First Amendment
that would result.

But Patriot, what stood out the most to me
was that, through all my opponents' bluster, it appears they've
learned nothing from the foreign policy failures of recent years.

Experience has shown us toppling secular
dictators in the Middle East hasn't worked.

The result has been chaos in Iraq, Libya and
Egypt. Radical Islamists filled the void.

ISIS is emboldened and on the march.

Yet, others are calling for more intervention
in Syria to topple yet another dictator, funneling weapons to
more radical Islamists.

The Utopian belief that we just wave a magic
wand and create freedom-loving democracies in the Middle East is
alive and well.

Facts don't matter to them. History doesn't
matter. Experience doesn't matter. Frankly, what you want for
the future of your country doesn't matter either.

Have they learned nothing?
What has America gotten in return?
Has it been worth it, Patriot?

Or do you believe we need a new strategy
based on common sense, reality and American interests?

While my opponents are clamoring for more
war, they steadfastly refuse to get serious about closing the
border, vetting those coming into our country or addressing our
broken visa program, which has let untold numbers of terrorists
in.
At the same time, they're all for
accelerating the number of dangerous Syrian refugees into our
country and putting these people on the government dole.

clean_body: Dear Patriot, There is no bigger question facing American voters in 2016 than who will best protect America. How we do it is the fundamental question of this election. Tonight's debate gave the American people a crystal clear picture of the differences between my views and those of my opponents. I'm already hearing "you won the debate" from every corner. But the support I'm most interested in right now is yours. Will you please contribute to my campaign's money bomb right away? As you saw from tonight's debate, Marco Rubio supports open borders and never-ending wars and government spying. He has more allegiance to Chuck Schumer and the Gang of Eight than conservative principles. Chris Christie is clamoring for World War III with reckless threats he has no intention of backing up. Donald Trump wants to shut down the Internet without understanding what that entails or the full frontal attack on the First Amendment that would result. But Patriot, what stood out the most to me was that, through all my opponents' bluster, it appears they've learned nothing from the foreign policy failures of recent years. Experience has shown us toppling secular dictators in the Middle East hasn't worked. The result has been chaos in Iraq, Libya and Egypt. Radical Islamists filled the void. ISIS is emboldened and on the march. Yet, others are calling for more intervention in Syria to topple yet another dictator, funneling weapons to more radical Islamists. The Utopian belief that we just wave a magic wand and create freedom-loving democracies in the Middle East is alive and well. Facts don't matter to them. History doesn't matter. Experience doesn't matter. Frankly, what you want for the future of your country doesn't matter either. Have they learned nothing? What has America gotten in return? Has it been worth it, Patriot? Or do you believe we need a new strategy based on common sense, reality and American interests? While my opponents are clamoring for more war, they steadfastly refuse to get serious about closing the border, vetting those coming into our country or addressing our broken visa program, which has let untold numbers of terrorists in. | | | | Peter, They all came at Marco and he knocked them down one by one. Tonight, the isolationist tag team duo Ted Cruz and Rand Paul tried to take on Marco. They got beat, badly. A great example: One of the most memorable moments of the debate was when Marco talked about his childhood in Nevada, hearing his grandfather talk about (while smoking one of his three daily cigars) how blessed Marco was to be up growing in America, a country where anyone can be anything if he works hard enough. _Marco's running for president because that just might not be true for the next generation: our kids and grandkids._ A candidate who can tell that story -- and has the ideas to follow through on restoring that promise -- is exactly what we need.
